title: The ferry that carries no cars
summary: A visitor to Wellington asks the captain of a small harbour ferry why the boat will never carry a single car.

body.speaker: Daniel
body.text: Captain, can I ask a question? Your ferry is small. Why does it only carry people? Why no cars?

body.speaker: Hemi
body.text: Look around the harbour. Do you see the big ferry over there?

body.speaker: Hemi
body.text: That ferry takes cars. It is wide. It is full of metal and oil. It is a useful boat. This boat is different.

body.speaker: Daniel
body.text: But you could earn more money with cars, no?

body.speaker: Hemi
body.text: Yes. But this boat is from 1962. Wooden floor. Small engine. If we put cars on it, the floor breaks in one year. Also, the people who come here do not want cars next to them. They want the wind. They want to talk.

body.speaker: Hemi
body.text: Workers in the morning. Students in the afternoon. Tourists like you in the middle. And on Sunday, families. Same families for many years. Grandparents bring their grandchildren on the same boat their grandparents brought them on.

body.speaker: Hemi
body.text: Never. The same three stops since 1962. Days Bay. Seatoun. Queens Wharf. That is it.

body.speaker: Hemi
body.text: Two of us. Me and a younger man named Pita. He has worked here for six years. I taught him. One day, when I retire, he will be the senior captain. He will teach someone else.

body.speaker: Daniel
body.text: And the boat? It will keep going?

body.speaker: Hemi
body.text: I hope so. It depends on people. If people choose this boat, the boat stays. If people choose only cars, the boat goes. The boat is not stronger than the choice.

annotations.meaningVi: phà / tàu chở khách qua nước
lớn tuổi / kỳ cựu (ở đây: trưởng)

grammarNotes.title: First conditional (real future)
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Cấu trúc 'If + present simple, will + V' nói về điều kiện có thể xảy ra trong tương lai và kết quả. Trong bài: 'If people choose this boat, the boat stays. If people choose only cars, the boat goes.' Ở đây, mệnh đề chính dùng hiện tại đơn ('stays', 'goes') vì người nói diễn đạt như một quy luật, nhưng cũng có thể viết 'will stay', 'will go'. Cả hai đều đúng trong văn nói.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The first conditional uses 'If + present simple, will + V' for real future possibilities. In the dialogue: 'If people choose this boat, the boat stays. If people choose only cars, the boat goes.' The main clause here uses the present simple ('stays', 'goes') because the speaker is framing it as a rule. 'Will stay' / 'will go' would also be correct.

translationVi:
Daniel: Bác ơi, cho tôi hỏi. Chuyến phà của bác nhỏ. Sao chỉ chở người? Sao không chở xe?
Hemi: Nhìn quanh cảng đi. Thấy chiếc phà lớn đằng kia không?
Daniel: Thấy. Chiếc trắng.
Hemi: Chiếc đó chở xe. Nó rộng. Đầy kim loại và dầu. Đó là phà có ích. Phà này khác.
Daniel: Nhưng chở xe thì kiếm được nhiều tiền hơn, phải không?
Hemi: Phải. Nhưng phà này từ năm 1962. Sàn gỗ. Máy nhỏ. Nếu chở xe, sàn vỡ trong một năm. Với lại, người tới đây không muốn xe sát mình. Họ muốn gió. Họ muốn trò chuyện.
Daniel: Ai đi phà này?
Hemi: Công nhân buổi sáng. Học sinh buổi chiều. Du khách như anh ở giữa ngày. Chủ Nhật thì các gia đình. Cùng những gia đình suốt nhiều năm. Ông bà đưa cháu lên chính chiếc phà mà ông bà của họ đã đưa họ.
Daniel: Tuyến đường có thay đổi không?
Hemi: Không bao giờ. Cùng ba điểm dừng từ 1962. Days Bay. Seatoun. Queens Wharf. Vậy thôi.
Daniel: Bác là thuyền trưởng duy nhất?
Hemi: Hai người. Tôi và một anh trẻ tên Pita. Cậu ấy làm sáu năm rồi. Tôi dạy cậu. Khi nào tôi nghỉ, cậu sẽ là thuyền trưởng chính. Cậu sẽ dạy người khác.
Daniel: Còn chiếc phà? Nó vẫn chạy chứ?
Hemi: Mong vậy. Tuỳ ở người ta. Nếu người ta chọn phà này thì phà ở lại. Nếu chọn chỉ xe thôi thì phà đi. Phà không mạnh hơn lựa chọn.

questions.prompt: What does the ferry NOT carry?
questions.explanation: 'Why does it only carry people? Why no cars?'

questions.prompt: What is the floor made of?
Why doesn't Hemi want to carry cars?
options: The wooden floor would break, and passengers want the wind, not cars beside them
Cars are too heavy to drive on
There is no road to the boat
questions.explanation: Two reasons given together in Hemi's reply.

questions.prompt: Who uses the ferry on Sunday?
What does Hemi say about the route?
options: It is decided by the mayor

questions.explanation: 'Me and a younger man named Pita.'

questions.prompt: How long has Pita worked there?
questions.explanation: 'He has worked here for six years.'

questions.prompt: What does Hemi say will decide if the boat keeps going?
options: The choices of the people who use it
questions.explanation: 'It depends on people. If people choose this boat, the boat stays.'

options: Small, specific things survive only when the people around them keep choosing them.
questions.explanation: Hemi's closing line frames it as a choice, not a fate.

cloze.template:
Daniel: Captain, can I ask a question? Your ferry is small. Why does it only carry {{b1}}? Why no cars?
Hemi: Look around the harbour. Do you see the big ferry over there?
Daniel: Yes. The white one.
Hemi: That ferry takes cars. It is wide. It is full of metal and oil. It is a useful boat. This boat is {{b2}}.
Daniel: But you could earn more money with cars, no?
Hemi: Yes. But this boat is from {{b3}}. Wooden floor. Small engine. If we put cars on it, the {{b4}} breaks in one year. Also, the people who come here do not want cars next to them. They want the {{b5}}. They want to talk.
Daniel: Who uses the ferry?
Hemi: Workers in the morning. Students in the afternoon. Tourists like you in the middle. And on Sunday, {{b6}}. Same families for many years. Grandparents bring their grandchildren on the same boat their grandparents brought them on.
Daniel: Has the route changed?
Hemi: Never. The same {{b7}} stops since 1962. Days Bay. Seatoun. Queens Wharf. That is it.
Daniel: Are you the only captain?
Hemi: Two of us. Me and a younger man named Pita. He has worked here for {{b8}} years. I taught him. One day, when I retire, he will be the senior captain. He will teach someone else.
Daniel: And the boat? It will keep going?
Hemi: I hope so. It depends on people. If people {{b9}} this boat, the boat stays. If people choose only cars, the boat goes. The boat is not stronger than the {{b10}}.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Hemi says the boat is 'not stronger than the choice.' Is there a small business, place, or service near you whose survival depends on whether ordinary people keep choosing it? What would happen if you started choosing it on purpose?
